fact: April 14 is exactly 104 days into the year - that's the same number as a full deck of cards plus both jokers!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for cherry blossoms to explode into pink clouds that make entire cities look like they're covered in cotton candy.
Why do so many baby animals seem extra bouncy right now? Spring energy is peaking, and young rabbits, lambs, and deer are literally hopping mad with excitement!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this time - nature's perfect compass is showing off its precision.
April 14 falls smack in the middle of Aries season, when the zodiac's ram is charging full speed ahead with fiery spring energy.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are turning brilliant orange and red right now - it's like the trees are putting on a fireworks show before winter.
Earthworms are having their busiest party of the year underground, churning up soil so fast they can process their own body weight in dirt every single day.
The night sky around April 14 showcases the constellation Leo the Lion prowling high overhead in the Northern Hemisphere - you can spot his backwards question mark mane!
Maple trees are literally bleeding sweet sap right now - but only in places where nights are still freezing while days warm up above 40°F.
This date sits exactly 261 days away from New Year's Day - more than 8 months of adventure still waiting in the year ahead!
Dandelions are secretly preparing their fuzzy seed parachutes around this time, getting ready to launch thousands of wishes into the wind.
In northern regions, ice on lakes makes the weirdest singing and groaning sounds as it finally starts cracking apart after months of winter freeze.
